svg.text: Dense semantic register with long definitions, status signals, owners, risks, and governance notes
Aligned view of systems, interfaces, ownership
the operating scope they belong to.
package, or system record used to validate
Condition that limits how an asset, parameter,
process step, or reusable module can be reused
Approved package that can be referenced by
workflow diagrams without rewriting the sourc...
Boundary where systems, teams, or process
owners exchange data, decisions, events, or
Named category in a hierarchy used to classify
freshness, approval status, or reuse readiness
Pointer to an out-of-bound system or provider
whose data influences the register but is not

g.text: Cyan: maps, interfaces, selected active domains
Yellow: governance, quality controls, active risk constraints
Green: approved reusable modules and reusable assets
Purple: external systems, optional links, out-of-bound references
Every term must resolve to one accountable owner and one evidence requirement.
Lifecycle state must describe current use permission, not project mood.
Long definitions should wrap inside cells without shrinking the register into unreadable
Cadence: monthly semantic review; immediate review on controlled-source change
Consumers: architecture, quality, document control, knowledge operations

svg.text: Stress template: long rows, semantic badges, and governance panels should remain readable without turning the table into a dashboard.
